title: Fix Recipe v1
description: Machine-applicable fix instruction emitted by the rule-set-enforcement-reviewer when a standards rule is violated. Consumed by TDD-022 code-fixer plugins. Schema source-of-truth for the FixRecipe interface in src/standards/fix-recipe.ts.

violation_id.description: Globally unique violation identifier. Format: VIO-<UTC-timestamp>-<8-hex-hash>.

rule_id.description: Namespaced standards rule ID per PLAN-021-1 (<plugin>:<id>).

file.description: Repo-relative path of the file the violation occurs in (or, for file-creation, the path the file SHOULD be created at).

line.description: 1-based line number of the violation; 0 for file-creation recipes (no existing line).

fix_type.description: Discriminator for the kind of fix. Determines how a downstream code-fixer interprets `before`/`after_template`.

before.description: The current state. For code-replacement: the offending text snippet. For file-creation: empty string. For dependency-add: the dependency name (used by the fixer to detect already-present).

after_template.description: The desired state. For code-replacement: the replacement snippet. For file-creation: the full file body. For dependency-add: the dependency declaration (e.g., a package.json/requirements.txt fragment).

confidence.description: Reviewer-asserted confidence the fix is correct without human review. 1.0 means fully automated; lower values warrant manual review.

manual_review_required.description: Optional explicit override. If true, downstream fixers MUST NOT auto-apply regardless of confidence.
